{"step_by_step_thinking": "The cranial part of the accessory nerve (CN XI) originates from the nucleus ambiguus and merges with the vagus nerve (CN X) to supply motor fibers to the larynx, pharynx, and soft palate. The stylopharyngeus muscle is innervated by the glossopharyngeal nerve (CN IX), not the vagus nerve (CN X) or the accessory nerve (CN XI). Therefore, the stylopharyngeus muscle would be spared if the cranial part of the accessory nerve is transected.", "answer_choice": "C"}
